page: Amazon Web Services r7iz.metal-16xl is a bare-metal, memory-optimized server powered by dedicated Intel Xeon 6455B processors. It features 32 physical cores and 64 vCPUs running at a high clock speed of 3.9 GHz, supported by a 60 MB L3 cache. The system is equipped with 512.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory operating at 5 GHz, providing 8.0 GB of RAM per physical core. It lacks local storage and dedicated GPU hardware, relying instead on network-attached storage and a baseline network bandwidth of 25 Gbps.

Benchmark results indicate strong multi-core and memory performance. The server achieves top-tier status in Geekbench single-core and multi-core tests, single-threaded gzip compression, and Passmark database operations. Memory bandwidth is highly efficient, scoring in the top 25% for both cached and uncached read/write operations. Additionally, the CPU's Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) and AVX-512 instruction sets contribute to strong performance in LLM inference, particularly for medium-sized 7B models where it reaches top-tier prompt processing and text generation speeds.

From a resource-density perspective, the r7iz.metal-16xl offers high qualitative cost efficiency for workloads requiring massive memory footprints combined with high-frequency CPU compute. However, the lack of local NVMe storage represents a key tradeoff, requiring reliance on network storage for persistent data. This configuration is highly suited for in-memory databases like Redis, heavy database operations, software compilation, and CPU-based machine learning inference.
description: Amazon Web Services r7iz.metal-16xl is a memory-optimized bare-metal server featuring 64 vCPUs powered by Intel Xeon 6455B processors running at 3.9 GHz. It is equipped with 512.0 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 5 GHz, though it lacks local storage and dedicated GPUs. The server delivers strong performance, ranking in the top 10% for Geekbench scores, single-threaded gzip compression, and Passmark database operations. It also demonstrates strong memory bandwidth and efficient CPU-based LLM inference via Intel AMX and AVX-512 extensions. While the absence of local storage requires network-attached alternatives, the high memory-to-core ratio and high-frequency CPU make this server highly efficient for in-memory databases, large-scale data processing, and enterprise database workloads.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r7iz.metal-16xl is a memory-optimized bare-metal server with 64 vCPUs, 512 GB of DDR5 RAM, and a 3.9 GHz Intel Xeon CPU, delivering top-tier database and memory performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services r7iz.metal-16xl is a bare-metal server with 64 vCPUs, 512 GB DDR5 RAM, and a 3.9 GHz Intel Xeon CPU for memory-heavy workloads.
tagline: A high-frequency bare-metal server combining 512 GB of DDR5 memory with advanced Intel Xeon processing for memory-intensive enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 64 vCPUs and 32 physical cores via Intel Xeon 6455B processors at 3.9 GHz
512.0 GB of high-speed 5 GHz DDR5 memory
Top-tier performance in database operations and single-threaded compression
Intel AMX and AVX-512 support for accelerated CPU-based LLM inference
No local storage, utilizing a 25 Gbps baseline network interface
Best suited for in-memory databases, software compilation, and data processing
